User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the CAPS Tutor application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user entity.

googleId.description: Google ID of the user for authentication.

gradeLevel.description: The current grade level of the user (1-12).

province.description: The province the user resides in.

school.description: The name of the user's school.

subjects.description: The subjects the user is studying.

literature.description: User's selected literature for their subjects.

Lesson.title: Lesson
Lesson.description: Represents a lesson within the CAPS syllabus.

id.description: Unique identifier for the lesson entity.

contentUrl.description: URL to the lesson content (e.g., PDF, HTML).

LearningObjective.title: LearningObjective
LearningObjective.description: Represents a specific learning objective within a lesson.

id.description: Unique identifier for the learning objective entity.

Question.title: Question
Question.description: Represents a question or exercise for a specific learning objective.

id.description: Unique identifier for the question entity.

type.description: Type of question (e.g., multiple choice, free text).

StudentProgress.title: StudentProgress
StudentProgress.description: Tracks a student's progress on a specific learning objective.

id.description: Unique identifier for the student progress entry.

completed.description: Indicates if the student has completed the learning objective.

masteryLevel.description: Mastery level of the student for the learning objective (e.g., percentage).

TeacherUploadedSyllabus.title: TeacherUploadedSyllabus
TeacherUploadedSyllabus.description: Represents syllabus content uploaded by a teacher.

id.description: Unique identifier for the uploaded syllabus content.

teacherId.description: Reference to User (the teacher uploading the content). (Relationship: User 1:N TeacherUploadedSyllabus)

fileUrl.description: URL where the uploaded file is stored.

gradeLevel.description: Grade level of the syllabus content (1-12).

uploadDate.description: Date and time the syllabus content was uploaded.

id.description: Unique identifier for the past paper entity.

teacherId.description: Reference to User (the teacher who uploaded the past paper). (Relationship: User 1:N PastPaper)

gradeLevel.description: Grade level of the past paper (1-12).

year.description: Year the past paper was administered.

fileUrl.description: URL where the past paper file is stored.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. The `userId` is the Firebase Authentication UID. Includes the user's gradeLevel and preferred subjects, which will be used to filter content on the home page.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the user.

definition.description: Tracks a student's progress on specific learning objectives. Stored under the user's document for path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the user.

params.name: studentProgressId
params.description: Unique identifier for the student progress entry.

definition.description: Stores lesson content.  Accessible based on gradeLevel. GradeLevel will affect which data is presented on the home page based on the user.
Stores learning objectives associated with a lesson.

params.name: lessonId
params.description: The ID of the parent Lesson.

params.name: learningObjectiveId
params.description: Unique identifier for the learning objective.

definition.description: Stores questions associated with a learning objective.

params.name: learningObjectiveId
params.description: The ID of the parent LearningObjective.

definition.description: Stores syllabus content uploaded by teachers. Stored under the user's document for path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the teacher (user).

params.name: teacherUploadedSyllabusId
params.description: Unique identifier for the uploaded syllabus content.

definition.description: Stores past exam papers uploaded by teachers. Stored as a top-level collection to be publicly readable.

params.name: pastPaperId
params.description: Unique identifier for the past paper.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to support the CAPS Tutor application's core features, including grade/subject selection, interactive practice, lesson hubs, adaptive exam generation, and progress dashboards. It incorporates Google sign-up/sign-in and adheres to the principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC, and QAPs. The structure prioritizes path-based ownership for user-specific data and denormalization of authorization data to avoid hierarchical dependencies.

**Authorization Independence (Denormalization):**
The structure utilizes denormalization to achieve authorization independence. For example, the `StudentProgress` collection is located under the user's document (`/users/{userId}/studentProgress/{studentProgressId}`), ensuring that access to a student's progress is directly tied to the user ID in the path, eliminating the need for `get()` calls to parent documents. The `gradeLevel` and `subjects` are stored in the User document, and will dictate the data presented on the home page.

**Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture):**
The structure segregates data based on access needs. User-specific data, like `StudentProgress` and uploaded content, are stored under the `/users/{userId}` path, while global data, like `Lessons` and `PastPapers`, are stored in top-level collections. This segregation simplifies security rules, as each collection has a clear and consistent security posture.

**Access Modeling:**
Path-Based Ownership: User-owned data (e.g., `StudentProgress`, `TeacherUploadedSyllabus`) is stored under the `/users/{userId}` path, providing clear ownership and simplified security rules.
Membership Map: Not explicitly used in this simplified structure, but could be added to lessons for collaborative editing by teachers if needed.
Global Roles (DBAC): Not implemented directly, but could be added using a dedicated `/roles_teacher/{uid}` collection to grant teacher roles.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**

Secure `list` operations are supported through structural segregation and path-based ownership. For example, listing student progress is secured by the `/users/{userId}/studentProgress` path, ensuring that only the logged-in user can access their own progress data, which will then affect home page data. Listing past papers is allowed for all authenticated users as it is a top-level collection.

**Invariants:**
Ownership: Path-based ownership enforces the invariant that a user owns their data (progress, uploaded content).
Timestamps: While not explicitly defined in all schemas, timestamps would be used to track creation and updates for entities like lessons and progress.
